slides.title: How fast is this function changing?
slides.body: Watch the arrow as it moves along the curve. Its direction shows the instant rate of change, or derivative, at each point, and a steeper arrow means a larger derivative.

slides.title: Where is the derivative greatest?
slides.body: Tap the point where the function is increasing fastest.

feedback.wrong: There is a point more steep than {point}

slides.title: Secant lines approximate the derivative
slides.body: Drag the two points along the curve. The green line is a secant; the purple curve is the function itself. Move the points together at one spot to see the tangent.

slides.title: Estimate the derivative
slides.body: Zoom in on the marked point until the curve looks like a straight line, then estimate the slope of that line. Give your best estimate of the derivative — it's fine if it's not exact.

feedback.wrong: The point of interest is at {x value to find derivative at}. Zoom in further. The curve should look almost straight. Recall that a derivative is the slope of the tangent line.

slides.title: The derivative as a limit
slides.body: Use the slider to choose point a on the curve. Then play the animation. A second point slides in from the right, and the secant slope approaches the tangent slope.

slides.title: Approach the fixed point
slides.body: Drag point P toward the fixed point at (2, 2) until the secant looks like a straight line through the curve, then estimate the slope of that line. Give your best estimate of the derivative — it's fine if it's not exact.

feedback.wrong: Drag P closer to the fixed point, then estimate the slope of the secant line.

slides.title: Critical points and zero slope
slides.body: Move the horizontal line until it touches the curve. Where it touches, the tangent is flat, so f′(x), the slope of f (its derivative), equals 0.

slides.title: Find every critical point
slides.body: The bottom graph shows f′(x). Tap every x-value where the derivative is zero. Those are the critical points of f.

feedback.wrong: Find every x where f′(x) = 0 on the derivative graph. Tap each zero crossing.
